item.title: Gulf Airspace Disruptions From Iran War Hits Seychelles Tourism
item.description: The number of visitors traveling to the Seychelles slumped 37% in March from a year ago, reflecting the impact of the Iran war and Middle East flight disruptions.

item.title: EU and US Near Critical Minerals Deal to Combat Chinese Control
item.description: The European Union and US are nearing an agreement to coordinate on producing and securing critical minerals, part of a push to break reliance on Chinese supplies.

item.title: Once-Popular UK Bond Trade Shaken by Surge in Volatility
item.description: The appeal of UK bonds is being put to the test by surging volatility, with money managers warning that one of the most popular trades in recent months now requires nerves of steel.

item.title: BMW India Head Sees Softer Luxury Car Demand on Gulf Turmoil
item.description: BMW AG’s India business is seeing affluent consumers turn more cautious as conflict in the Persian Gulf weighs on sentiment and threatens to weaken local demand for luxury cars.

item.title: How Shipping Insurance Really Works During a War
item.description: How marine insurers became crucial to global trade.

item.title: Trapped French Property Fund Investors Find an Exit, at a Price
item.description: As a growing number of French property funds freeze withdrawals in the face of a client exodus that’s overwhelmed their cash buffers, the true cost of liquidity is becoming clearer.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Shipping Insurance Works During a War (Podcast)
item.description: When the conflict with Iran started, some of the first headlines we saw had to do with shipping insurance. Marine insurers were said to be canceling war risk coverage for vessels going through the Strait of Hormuz. Premiums were said to surge. Meanwhile, the Trump administration announced it would offer its own insurance for ships traversing the Gulf, in an effort to get things moving again. So why is insurance such a crucial part of maritime trade? And how does the system actually work? In this

item.title: Fosun Weighs Options to Sell 20.45% Stake in BCP, Expresso Says
item.description: Chinese conglomerate Fosun International is weighing options for its 20.45% stake in Banco Comercial Português SA including a sale, Expresso reported, as it steps up efforts to raise cash and streamline its portfolio.

item.title: Russian-Flagged Tanker Transits Hormuz Into Gulf in Rare Passage
item.description: A Russian-flagged supertanker passed through the Strait of Hormuz into the Persian Gulf, marking a rare passage for one of the nation’s ships as traders scrutinize every transit through the war-hit waterway.

item.title: Indian Bonds Fall as RBI to Drain Cash in First Move This Year
item.description: India’s sovereign bond yields rose after the central bank announced its first step this year to drain cash from the banking system, as it seeks to push up overnight borrowing costs to its policy rate.

item.title: Indonesia Deploys Thousands of African Bugs to Boost Palm Output
item.description: Thousands of tiny bugs from Africa have been released at an Indonesian palm oil plantation in North Sumatra as the world’s biggest grower seeks to revive output growth after years of stalling production.

item.title: Global Funds Flow Back Into Asian AI Stocks as War Tensions Ease
item.description: As tensions in the Middle East begin to ease, global investors are turning their attention back to the artificial intelligence trade, funneling fresh funds into Asian tech stocks after weeks of selling.

item.title: Porsche Sales Tumble on China Slump, Model Changeovers
item.description: Porsche AG’s first-quarter sales fell after a steep drop in China, where luxury spending remains subdued, compounding pressure from model transitions and slower demand for electrified cars in the US.

item.title: Turkish Airlines Replaces Management, Names New CEO and Chairman
item.description: Turkish Airlines carried out a sweeping management overhaul by replacing its chief executive officer and chairman, installing a new leadership team as the global aviation industry grapples with flight restrictions and high fuel costs.

item.title: India Equity Fund Inflows Hit Second-Highest on Record in March
item.description: India’s stock mutual fund inflows accelerated to the second-highest on record in March, showing resilience among individual investors despite heightened geopolitical tensions and market volatility.

item.title: Turkey Overhauls Leadership at State Lenders Halkbank, Vakifbank
item.description: Turkey reshuffled the top ranks of two of its state-owned banks, installing new leadership at Turkiye Halk Bankasi and transferring its outgoing chief executive to Turkiye Vakiflar Bankasi, according to exchange filings late Thursday.

item.title: SoftBank Group Prices ¥418 Billion Retail Bonds at Record 4.97%
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. priced yen hybrid bonds targeting retail investors at an initial five-year coupon of 4.97%, marking the highest level for such debt issued by the company.

item.title: Orban Warns of ‘Danger’ If Hungarians Oust Him in Elections
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban addressed the nation two days before pivotal elections, telling voters that he was the safe bet and that ousting him after 16 years in office, as independent polls suggest may happen, would imperil the nation.

item.title: China AI Firm Discloses $92 Million of Banned Nvidia Chip Servers to Beijing
item.description: Hours after the United States charged a Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder with illegally smuggling billions of dollars’ worth of Nvidia Corp. AI chips to China, shares of a little-known Shenzhen-based computing company plummeted by the daily limit of 20%.

item.title: Stocks Set to Rise Ahead of US-Iran Weekend Talks
TSMC’s Sales Beat Estimates After War Fails to Dent AI Demand
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 35% increase in quarterly revenue, suggesting global AI chip demand remained intact during the first weeks of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Indonesia’s ‘Gasoline Godfather’ a Suspect in Second Graft Case
item.description: Indonesia has named reclusive commodities merchant Mohammad Riza Chalid as a suspect in a second corruption case tied to crude oil and fuel procurement, putting renewed scrutiny on legacy practices in one of Southeast Asia’s largest energy markets.

item.title: Indonesia’s Richest Man Sells Stakes as Ownership Rules Tighten
item.description: Indonesia’s richest man has begun selling small stakes in his listed companies as tighter ownership rules push firms to increase shares available to public investors.

item.title: Indian Rupee Leads Asia Gains After RBI Cracks Down on Speculation
item.description: The Indian rupee has become Asia’s best-performing currency since the central bank cracked down on speculation late last month, with most positions unwound ahead of Friday’s deadline, traders said.

item.title: China Reminds Battery Makers to Resist Excessive Capacity Growth
item.description: China summoned the country’s leading battery makers for a second time in a little over three months, reinforcing a call to restrict capacity expansion and avoid the price wars that have damaged other renewable-energy industries in the past.

item.title: Credit Fund Manager Says Africa Looks Safer as Global Wars Rise
item.description: Wars in the Middle East and Ukraine are reshaping investor risk perceptions, highlighting Africa as more resilient than its reputation suggests, according to London-based credit fund manager TLG Capital Ltd.

item.title: Stealth Alibaba Video AI Model Tops Global Ranking on Debut
item.description: A mysterious video generation model that swept to the top of global benchmarks was developed by a team under Alibaba Group Holding Ltd., sending ripples across China’s AI industry.

item.title: China Battery Storage Maker Sees Profit Boost from Iran Upheaval
item.description: A top Chinese battery storage manufacturer said it expects a sharp rise in first-quarter profit as overseas demand surges, a sign the country’s clean tech exporters stand to benefit from energy disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Nvidia-Backed Lumentum Sees AI Filling Orders Through 2028
item.description: Lumentum Holdings Inc. said demand from US hyperscalers for its optical components is accelerating and on track to fill its order book through 2028.

item.title: Oaktree Says Exposure to Software Firms, Direct Lending Is Small
item.description: Oaktree Capital Management assured clients its exposure to software companies and direct lending remains limited, as global private credit funds retreat from areas showing heightened risk.
